intro: Harrisia pomanensis, also known as the Poma cactus or Poma Harrisia, is a species of flowering cactus native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Harrisia pomanensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoid saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Harrisia pomanensis is commonly used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are believed to aid in the treatment of various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
